zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Anne Auder於1504年出生在英國坎布利其爾時，她的父親George Auder13歲，母親Emma Turner14歲。她與Richard Cox結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1568年在她的家鄉享年64歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾可拉維爾頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Anne Auder于1504年出生在英国坎布利其爾时，她的父亲George Auder13岁，母亲Emma Turner14岁。她与Richard Cox结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1568年在她的家乡享年64岁，埋葬在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾可拉維爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Auder
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Sauter  . Compare   Souder   and   Sowder  .

variants.name: Sowder
meanings: Americanized form of German   Sauder   or   Sauter  .

variants.name: Sauter
meanings: German: occupational name for a shoemaker or cobbler (one who sews leather and other materials), Middle High German sūter (from Latin sutor, an agent derivative of suere ‘to sew’). Compare Sauder and Souter .
Dutch and Flemish: occupational name for a producer or seller of salt, from an agent derivative of Middle Dutch sout ‘salt’.

variants.name: Souder
meanings: In some cases also Breton (Côtes-d'Armor; mainly Le Souder): variant of Soudéer, an occupational name for a mercenary soldier or a hired servant, from Old French soldoier.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Anne Auder was born in 1504, in Cambridgeshire, England, her father, George Auder, was 13 and her mother, Emma Turner, was 14. She married Richard Cox. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1568, in her hometown, at the age of 64, and was buried in Claverdon, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Anne Auder est née en 1504, à Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, son père, George Auder, avait 13 ans et sa mère, Emma Turner, avait 14 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Cox. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en 1568, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Claverdon, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Anne Auder 1504 in Cambridgeshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater George Auder 13 und ihre Mutter Emma Turner 14. Sie hat Richard Cox geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb 1568 im Alter von 64 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Claverdon, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Anne Auder è nata il nel 1504 nel Cambridgeshire, England, sua padre, George Auder, aveva 13 anni e sua madre, Emma Turner, aveva 14 anni. Lei sposato Richard Cox. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1568, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolta a Claverdon, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Anne Auderが1504年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアで生まれた時，父George Auderは13歳，母Emma Turnerは14歳であった。Richard Coxと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1568年に故郷で64歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアクラバードンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Anne Auder(이)가 1504년 영국 캄브리지실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 George Auder(은)는 13세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Emma Turner(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 Richard Cox(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1568년 그녀의 고향에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 클레이버든에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Anne Auder nasceu em 1504, em Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, George Auder, tinha 13 anos e sua mãe, Emma Turner, tinha 14 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard Cox. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1568, em sua cidade natal, com 64 anos, e foi sepultada em Claverdon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Anne Auder родилась в 1504 г. в местности камбриджешир, Англия, её отцу по имени George Auder было 13 лет, а матери, которую звали Emma Turner, 14. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Cox. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1568 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности клавирдон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Anne Auder nació en 1504, en Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, su papá, George Auder, tenía 13 años y su mamá, Emma Turner, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Richard Cox. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1568, en su pueblo, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Claverdon, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
